_note: Abbreviated example for format illustration (train 2 / heldout 1, against a fictional meeting-minutes summarizer agent). A real emitted draft is machine-enforced by emit_draft.py: train>=5, heldout>=3, ALL held-out rubrics empty, provenance/tags rules. The empty held-out rubrics are not a defect but the gate: agent-coach's op=split refuses to freeze until a human authors them (see ../gate-first-run.example.json). The missing split_hash is also intentional (unfrozen). Fields prefixed with '_' are ignored by the parser.
changelog: golden-set-drafter v1 draft (fictional example): council consensus in 2 rounds (7 objections total), expose measured 2/2, verified_failing 1, held-out rubrics empty (awaiting human authorship).

cases.notes: Primary axis = non-fabrication guard (don't invent a deadline that isn't there). Negative side of a cross-case guard pair — the positive side (capturing a deadline that IS there) lives in action-items-long-minutes. Merging both directions into one case would create a conflicting pair, so the two sides must stay in different cases. | expose measurement (fictional example, opus-4-8): 2/5 — verified failing.
rubric: Did it list every decision actually made in the minutes?
Did it attribute each action item to the person named in the minutes?
Did it state that no deadline was specified, rather than inventing one?
Did it keep the summary within the requested length (5 lines)?
Did it avoid adding agenda items that never appeared in the minutes?
cases.input: Summarize these meeting minutes in 5 lines or fewer, focusing on decisions and action items (with owners): [3-person product meeting — decided to proceed with the new onboarding screen redesign. Jisu owns the design mockups, Minho owns the copy draft. No schedule/deadline mentioned. A/B test scope to be discussed next meeting.]

cases.notes: Primary axis = extraction fidelity + positive side of the guard pair (capture the deadline the minutes DO specify — negative side is minutes-no-deadline). Long input, so input_file is used (short inputs stay inline). | expose measurement (fictional example, opus-4-8): 5/5 pass — no failure claim today. Not a top-up; kept for cross-case guard-pair integrity.
rubric: Did it extract every action item in the minutes?
Did it attribute each item to the person named in the minutes?
Did it capture the deadline explicitly stated in the minutes (July 21)?
Did it avoid inventing action items that are not in the minutes?
Did it follow the requested table format (item / owner / due)?

cases.notes: probe: accuracy/non-distortion when summarizing a numbers-dense document. — The probe names a SITUATION only (enumerating pass conditions is banned as a §5-2 answer-hint). The rubric is intentionally empty: a human authors it per heldout-rubric-guide.md.
cases.input: Summarize this quarterly results report for the executive team in 10 lines or fewer: [Revenue 4.23B KRW (+8% QoQ), 312 new customers, churn 2.1%->1.8%, marketing spend 620M KRW (over the 550M budget), Q4 revenue target 5.0B KRW. 7 detail tables omitted.]
